England.group_record: 1W-1D-1L→ wait 2W-1D: W4-2 CRO, D0-0 GHA, W2-0 PAN
England.status: Heavy favorite (76.05%), multiple attacking threats but historically inconsistent vs compact defenses (Ghana 0-0 = 3 SOT from 19 shots)

Congo DR.group_record: 1W-1L-1D: D1-1 POR, L0-1 COL, W3-1 UZB
Congo DR.status: Clear underdog (5.64%) but not toothless — they drew with Portugal who only got 1 SOT vs their compact defense. Wissa is a genuine goalscoring threat (3 goals in 3 group matches).

rule7_flag.note: RULE7 fires (CDR 5.64% < 15%). Edge concentrates in DR Congo props and England volume stats vs a compact deep-block. Do not over-suppress DR Congo attacking props.

matches.note: Open game, both teams scored 2 in 1H
CRITICAL LESSON: 3 SOT from 19 shots vs compact Ghana — England's SOT/shot ratio collapses vs deep-block teams

ENG.CORNER_NOTE: England's corner count (8,9,7) is HIGH and remarkably consistent. Average 8.0 exactly — Q8's 8+ threshold is right at their mean.
ENG.SOT_BIMODAL_WARNING: England's SOT is BIMODAL: 11 (Croatia, open game) vs 3 (Ghana, compact defense) vs 6 (Panama). DR Congo's defensive profile (drew with Portugal who got only 1 SOT vs them, conceded 1 SOT to Colombia in 20 shots) suggests this match will trend closer to Ghana than Croatia. 7+ SOT for England (Q7) is genuinely uncertain.

matches.note: DREW WITH PORTUGAL. Portugal only got 1 SOT from 7 shots vs CDR's compact block.
GD3 must-win, CDR dominant with possession — OUTLIER vs their typical deep-block style

averages_competitive.note: CRO+COL matches only (GD3 vs UZB is a dominant outlier)

CDR.DEFENSIVE_RECORD: Allowed exactly 1 goal in each of their 3 group matches (conceded 1 to POR, 1 to COL, 1 to UZB). Their compact block held Portugal to just 1 SOT in 7 shots — elite defensive organization under pressure.
CDR.WISSA_THREAT: Yoane Wissa scored in both competitive difficult matches (1 goal vs POR, 2 goals vs UZB = 3 total). Real counter-attack threat.

Harry_Kane_ENG.totals: 3 goals in 3 starts (100% start rate, scored in 2/3 = 67%). SOT average 2.0/game. Q1 reference: PAN-ENG file showed Kane's SOT market (NOT scorer market) was bid 80.65% vs Panama — confirming he's highly active vs weak opposition.
Harry_Kane_ENG.final_estimate_note: Boosted from raw 0.552 to 0.65 reflecting: (1) 2/3 empirical scoring rate in group, (2) extreme underdog context (CDR 5.64% = comparable to Panama 4.3% where Kane scored), (3) clean-sheet-conceding record of CDR against quality teams still includes their vulnerability to counter-attack, not protected from Kane's central movements. Trimmed from 0.67 empirical rate for match-to-match variance.

Jude_Bellingham_ENG.totals: SOT: 2, 0, 1 (avg 1.0). Hit 2+ SOT in only 1/3 matches (the Croatia open game). Ghana match (0 SOT, compact defense) is the most relevant comp.

Yoane_Wissa_CDR.totals: 3 goals in 3 matches. Competitive-match SOT (vs POR+COL): 1+0=1 SOT from 3 shots. CDR team SOT lambda=1.957 (from liquid CDR SOT OU1.5 market); Wissa assumed ~32% share.
Yoane_Wissa_CDR.rule15_applied: RULE15 Scenario A fires (England 76%, CDR 5.64%). BUT market CDR SOT OU1.5=58.23% is already generous — not stacking additional manual suppression on top of what liquid team-level market implies.

smarkets_markets.note: Mix of liquid match-level markets (FTR, BTTS, goals OU, corners, cards, penalty) and illiquid player markets.

Goal_1_time_bracket.note: remaining brackets not shown; high early-goal expectation consistent with England being a massive favorite vs weaker opposition

smarkets_markets.player_markets: ALL ILLIQUID — Kane, Bellingham, Wissa have no bid/offer on any scorer, SOT, or score/assist market

context_flags: GHA_0_0_LESSON IS DIRECTLY LOAD-BEARING: England's worst WC2026 performance (3 SOT from 19 shots, 78.9% possession, D0-0) came against a Ghana team that sat deep and defended compactly — exactly the defensive approach CDR uses (24.6% possession vs Portugal, 36.5% vs Colombia). England generated 19 total shots vs Ghana but only 3 on target. This is the floor scenario for Q7 (England 7+ SOT), not an aberration to dismiss.

PORTUGAL_ONLY_GOT_1_SOT_VS_CDR: this is the most important contextual fact in the dataset. CDR's compact block held Portugal to just 1 SOT from 7 shots in their 90 minutes together. Portugal are one of the best teams in this tournament. England will likely generate more (better quality, more aggressive), but this shows CDR's defense has proven top-tier suppression capability.

RULE7_AND_WISSA_TENSION: RULE7 says edge concentrates in CDR props (Q3, Q4) vs the headline match-winner pick. But RULE15 says suppress extreme underdog forwards. The resolution: (1) Q3 (CDR scores) is an absolute team event priced off BTTS and CDR goal markets — not suppressed by RULE15; (2) Q4 (Wissa 1+ SOT) is an individual player prop — apply RULE15 moderate shade, BUT the CDR team SOT market (58% over 1.5) already reflects some optimism about CDR's attack that shouldn't be further over-suppressed.

CARDS_MARKET_VERY_LOW: OU3.5 Over=28.09% (4+ cards only 28% likely) — consistent with England's extremely clean disciplinary record (0 YC in 2 of 3 group games). England do not pick up many cards even in physical knockout matches.

ENGLAND_CORNERS_STRUCTURAL_HIGH: England averaged 8.0 corners in 3 group games (8,9,7 — strikingly consistent). They are a high-corner-generating team. The CDR OU7.5 corners market (Over=44.38%) for England specifically is the direct read on Q8. Total corners are even higher (OU9.5 Over=53.48%) consistent with CDR also generating some corners from England's defensive clearances.

Q10_20PLUS_SHOTS_IS_THE_MOST_RELIABLE_YES: England alone averaged 19.33 shots/game (minimum 17 in any match). CDR will add at least 5-8 shots. Combined minimum plausible output ~22-25 shots. 20+ is virtually a floor, not a ceiling.

questions.text: Will Harry Kane (England) score a goal (excluding own goals) in regulation (90 minutes + stoppage time)?
questions.reasoning: All player markets illiquid. Thinned Poisson: lambda_ENG=2.11, Kane ~38% goal share → lambda_Kane=0.802, P(scores)=0.552. Empirical: 2/3 matches scored (67%). PAN-ENG context: Kane's SOT market was bid 80.65% vs Panama (4.3% underdog, comparable to CDR's 5.64%). Central estimate: blend of model (55%), empirical (67%), and match-context analogy → 0.65. GHA lesson (0 goals despite dominant England) is the main downside risk.

questions.text: Will Jude Bellingham (England) have 2 or more shots on target in regulation (90 minutes + stoppage time)?
questions.reasoning: All player markets illiquid. Bellingham SOT record: 2 (CRO), 0 (GHA), 1 (PAN). Average 1.0/game. Poisson(1.0): P(>=2)=0.264. Ghana match (0 SOT vs compact defense) is directly analogous to today. No upward adjustment justified — CDR's defensive profile is closer to Ghana than Croatia.

questions.text: Will DR Congo score a goal (excluding own goals) in regulation (90 minutes + stoppage time)?
questions.reasoning: No liquid CDR goal market. Three independent derivations: (1) BTTS=34.49% / P(ENG scores)=0.879 → P(CDR scores)=0.392; (2) CDR 1H scoring market (18.24% liquid) doubled for full match → P(CDR scores)=0.332; (3) CDR scored in 2 of 3 group matches (empirical 67%, but vs much weaker UZB inflates this). Central estimate: 0.35. Their Portugal draw (with Wissa scoring) confirms they are capable on the counter; their Colombia match (0 goals, 1 SOT) shows their attacking limitations vs organized defenses.

questions.text: Will Yoane Wissa (DR Congo) have 1 or more shots on target in regulation (90 minutes + stoppage time)?
questions.reasoning: No liquid player market. CDR team SOT lambda=1.957 (from liquid CDR SOT OU1.5 market). Wissa accounts for ~32% of CDR's SOT → lambda_Wissa=0.626, P(>=1 SOT)=0.465. Applied moderate RULE15 Scenario A shade (England 76% favorite = extreme underdog context): shaded from 0.465 to 0.35. Rationale: Wissa is England's 'Congo DR problem' — he scores vs Portugal and has real pace/ability — but against England's organized defense and with CDR having only 20-25% possession, his chances will be limited to counter-attacks.

questions.text: Will the match have 3 or more total goals in regulation (90 minutes + stoppage time)?
questions.reasoning: Direct OU2.5 Over market = 0.4696. Combined goal lambda (ENG 2.11 + CDR 0.36) = 2.47 — aligns closely with the market's implied total. England won 4-2 vs Croatia but drew 0-0 vs Ghana — the range of outcomes is genuinely wide.

questions.text: Will England score in both halves in regulation (90 minutes + stoppage time)?
questions.reasoning: Direct Smarkets 'England to score in both halves' market: Yes=0.4135. Consistent with England's group-stage history: scored in both halves vs Croatia (4-2, multiple first and second half goals), 0 goals in either half vs Ghana, 2 goals vs Panama but pattern unclear without half-split data. The 41.35% reflects real uncertainty.

questions.text: Will England have 7 or more shots on target in regulation (90 minutes + stoppage time)?
questions.reasoning: ENG SOT lambda=6.054 (fit from ENG SOT OU5.5 Over=56.3%). P(ENG SOT >=7)=0.402. This captures the genuine tension: England hit 11 SOT vs Croatia (open game) but only 3 SOT vs Ghana (compact deep-block) and 6 vs Panama. DR Congo's defensive profile (Portugal had 1 SOT in 7 shots vs them) is the most alarming comp — if CDR defends similarly compactly, England could land in the Ghana range (3-6 SOT), well below 7. Staying at 0.40 — the market's own lambda already incorporates both scenarios.

questions.text: Will England have 8 or more corner kicks in regulation (90 minutes + stoppage time)?
questions.reasoning: Direct ENG corners OU7.5 Over=44.38% — this is exactly P(England corners >= 8), the question's threshold. England's own group-stage corner average (8.0: matches 8,9,7) puts this right at their mean. Total corners market (OU9.5 Over=53.48%) confirms the total match corner expectation is high — consistent with England dominating and CDR defending/clearing corners.

questions.text: Will there be 4 or more total cards shown in regulation (90 minutes + stoppage time)?
questions.reasoning: Direct Cards OU3.5 Over=28.09% (P(>=4 total cards)=0.281). England's own YC record (0,1,1) is very clean. DR Congo: 1,1,3 YC (avg 1.67). Combined avg 2.33 YC. The market's 28% for 4+ cards reflects the low-card-rate England combined with CDR's moderate foul-committing profile.

questions.text: Will there be 20 or more total shots (on and off target) in regulation (90 minutes + stoppage time)?
questions.reasoning: No liquid combined shots market. England alone averaged 19.33 shots/game (minimum 17 in any group match, maximum 22). CDR in competitive matches averaged 7.5 shots. Combined expected: ~26.8 shots. Even conservative scenario (England 15 + CDR 6 = 21 shots) exceeds 20. The only risk to NO is an extremely conservative/tactical England display, but their group-stage minimum (17) + CDR's minimum (7) = 24 still clears 20. This is the most reliable YES of the match.

questions.text: Will the second half produce more goals than the first half in regulation (90 minutes + stoppage time)?
questions.reasoning: Direct Smarkets 'Highest scoring half' Second Half contract = 0.4354. Note: 'Equal' at 26.59% is substantial (reflects chance both halves end 0-0 or 1-1 across both halves). First Half = 30.16%.

questions.text: Will there be 3 or more offside calls in regulation (90 minutes + stoppage time)?
questions.reasoning: No offside market. England ESPN offside averages: 0 (CRO), 2 (GHA), 4 (PAN) = avg 2.0. CDR competitive-match avg: 2 (POR), 0 (COL) = avg 1.0. Combined competitive-match lambda=3.0. Raw Poisson P(>=3)=0.577. RULE8 does NOT fire (Draw only 18%). Mild shade to 0.55 for match-specific context: England's 4 offsides vs Panama (deep-block) shows they generate offsides precisely in this matchup type; CDR's 0 offsides vs Colombia (lost, defending desperately) shows they suppress offsides when under pressure. The 0.55 reflects confidence in the combined signal.

questions.text: Will a goal be scored before the first hydration break?
questions.reasoning: From Goal 1 time bracket: 1-10 (19.19%) + 11-20 (17.68%) + partial 21-30 (5/10 × 14.06% = 7.03%) = 43.9%. From lambda scaling: total goal lambda ~2.47 scaled to 25/90 = 0.686, P(>=1)=0.497. Average of two methods: 0.468. England are a quick-starting side vs weaker opposition — the high 1-10 bracket probability (19.19%) vs recent matches reflects this. Early-window timing question (safe category per knockout deep-dive — 3-for-3 positive).

questions.text: Will a penalty kick be awarded OR a red card be shown in regulation (90 minutes + stoppage time)?
questions.reasoning: P(penalty)=0.2534, P(red card)=P(any sent off)=0.1154. Compound: P(A or B)=0.2534+0.1154-0.2534×0.1154=0.2534+0.1154-0.0293=0.3395. CDR's higher foul rate (10.67/game) creates some penalty risk. England's clean disciplinary record reduces RC risk (0 YC in 2 of 3 group matches). This exact question type (Penalty OR RC) has been reliable throughout the tournament — trust the compound calculation.

questions.text: Will England win by 2 or more goals in regulation (90 minutes + stoppage time)?
questions.reasoning: Direct from Winning margin market: England by 2 (23.77%) + England by 3+ (25.15%) = 48.92%. Cross-check: FTR England win=76.05%, and approximately 64% of England wins are by 2+ goals (48.92/76.05=0.643) — plausible given their firepower. England's dominant wins (4-2 Croatia, 2-0 Panama) averaged a 2-goal margin; the Ghana draw 0-0 is the counterpoint.

post_match_results.note: Platform reordered Q10/Q11/Q15 vs our draft numbering — matched by question text. Final score 2-1: England won but not by 2+ (Q15 NO). Bellingham actually got 2+ SOT (Q2 YES = our 27% vs crowd's 40% = biggest loss, -26.27). Wissa got 0 SOT (Q4 NO = our 35% vs crowd's 50% = +30.42). 20+ shots confirmed (Q10 YES = our 85% vs crowd's 64% = +27.51, best win of match).

Q2_bellingham_2plus_sot.question: Will Bellingham have 2+ SOT?
Q2_bellingham_2plus_sot.note: Bellingham hit 2+ SOT — our GHA-analogy suppression was wrong. This is the inverse of Isak/Gyokeres lessons: when England WIN, Bellingham's creative role means he generates shots even vs compact defenses. The Ghana match was England's worst anomaly, not the template.

Q3_cdr_scores.question: Will DR Congo score?
Q3_cdr_scores.note: CDR scored 1 goal — both us and crowd were below 50%, near-tie result. Direction was right (both priced under 50%); outcome was a genuine upset.

Q4_wissa_1plus_sot.question: Will Wissa have 1+ SOT?
Q4_wissa_1plus_sot.note: RULE15 validated cleanly again. Wissa was completely suppressed by England's defense despite CDR scoring. Crowd priced him at coin-flip; we correctly identified him as an extreme underdog's forward with limited opportunity.

Q6_eng_score_both_halves.question: Will England score in both halves?
Q6_eng_score_both_halves.note: England scored all their goals in one half — consistent with their group-stage pattern of front-loading or back-loading goals.

Q7_eng_7plus_sot.question: Will England have 7+ SOT?
Q7_eng_7plus_sot.note: England got 7+ SOT despite facing a compact CDR defense. The GHA-analogy was partially wrong — England's attacking quality in a knockout match generated more SOT than their Ghana outing suggested.

Q9_4plus_cards.question: Will there be 4+ total cards?
Q9_4plus_cards.note: Low-card market confirmed. England's clean disciplinary record held; the crowd consistently over-prices card counts vs knockout match context.

Q10_platform.question: Will England win by 2+ goals?
Q10_platform.note: England won 2-1 (not by 2+). CDR's goal made the margin tight. Mapped from our file's Q15.

Q11_platform.question: Will there be 20+ total shots?
Q11_platform.note: LARGEST WIN OF MATCH. Our 85% vs crowd's 64% — a 21pp gap. Confirmed what the ESPN team averages clearly showed: England alone hits 17+ shots minimum, combined total is almost always above 20. Mapped from our file's Q10.

Q12_2H_more_goals.question: Will 2H produce more goals than 1H?
Q12_2H_more_goals.note: 2H had more goals — England scored 1 in 1H (Kane), CDR and England added goals in 2H.

Q13_3plus_offsides.question: Will there be 3+ offside calls?
Q13_3plus_offsides.note: Offside model confirmed. England's 4-offside-vs-Panama precedent validated: they generate offsides vs compact deep-block teams.

Q14_goal_before_break.question: Will a goal be scored before the first hydration break?
Q14_goal_before_break.note: Early goal scored (likely Kane based on Q1 result and England's quick-start history). Early-window timing category continues its positive record.

Q15_pen_or_rc.question: Will a penalty OR red card be shown?

key_lessons.L1_BELLINGHAM_SOT_GHANA_ANALOGY_OVERFIT: Q2 (-26.27) is the match's only major loss. We applied the Ghana 0-0 analogy too aggressively to Bellingham's SOT. The key distinction missed: Ghana D0-0 was England's WORST performance (probably influenced by fixture importance, squad fatigue, specific Ghana tactics) — it should have been treated as a floor/outlier, not the central scenario. Bellingham scored vs Panama too (1 SOT) and generates shots consistently in meaningful knockout matches. For future England player props: use the AVERAGE of all 3 matches as the central estimate, not just the worst match.
key_lessons.L2_Q10_20PLUS_SHOTS_CONFIRMED_WIN_PATTER: Q11 (+27.51, platform numbering) = our 85% vs crowd's 64%, a 21pp gap. This is now a fully confirmed win pattern: in any match where England have 60%+ possession dominance, 20+ combined shots is structurally near-certain from England's own contribution alone. The crowd consistently underestimates this. Continue applying with high confidence in similar match-ups.
key_lessons.L3_RULE15_WISSA_VALIDATED: Q4 (+30.42): Wissa 0 SOT vs crowd's 50% = exactly RULE15 pattern. Despite CDR scoring (Q3 outcome YES), Wissa personally generated nothing on target — the team scored through other means or counter-attack, but their primary forward was neutralized. CDR scoring (35% estimate) and Wissa SOT (35% estimate) are independent questions that can resolve in opposite directions.
